nodes.title: Reject controls and backslashes before trimming
nodes.why: Removing a control can expose a dangerous scheme, while backslashes can become slashes under special-URL parsing.
nodes.alternative: Strip or repair suspicious characters and continue.
nodes.tradeoff: Rejecting ambiguous input is stricter but produces one stable interpretation.

nodes.title: Reject encoded controls and protocol-relative input
nodes.why: These forms are unnecessary for the documented href contract and can hide parser-sensitive input.
nodes.tradeoff: Some unusual paths are rejected in exchange for an unambiguous policy.

nodes.title: Parse explicit URLs directly and relatives against a trusted base

anchor.snippet: isExplicit ? new URL(value) : new URL(value, TRUSTED_BASE)

nodes.why: Parsing must match browser URL semantics before scheme and origin policy is applied.
nodes.alternative: Use string prefixes as the final security decision.
nodes.tradeoff: URL parsing adds a small cost but validates the browser's interpretation.

nodes.title: Allowlist the final parsed protocol
nodes.why: The parsed protocol is the relevant XSS boundary, including mixed-case input.
nodes.alternative: Maintain a denylist of known dangerous schemes.

nodes.title: Keep every relative URL on the trusted origin
nodes.why: Resolving against a reserved base makes the same-origin relative policy independent of the deployment hostname.
nodes.alternative: Assume every scheme-less string is a local path.
nodes.tradeoff: An explicit origin comparison prevents parser edge cases from escaping the relative policy.
